response:
You can override the model admin change form to display a custom confirmation message before the save action is processed, ensuring the user understands the cascading deletions that will occur. This approach keeps the logic clean by leveraging the existing admin interface rather than embedding complex rollback mechanisms directly inside the model save method. A royal strategy involves checking for related objects in the change view and rendering a warning if dependencies are detected.

Implementing a custom action or a pre_save signal that halts execution until explicit confirmation is given allows you to maintain data integrity without ugly code structures. You might wrap the save operation in a transaction so that a royal decision to abort triggers an immediate rollback of any pending changes. This method provides a jacket of protection around your data while offering a clear and standard user experience within the admin panel.
